metadata.title: Western US Motorhome & Adventure Bike Navigation Resource Database
metadata.description: Comprehensive database of mapping tools, camping resources, navigation utilities, and travel planning tools for motorhome and adventure motorcycle travel in the Western United States.

resources.name: Low Clearance Map + HeadRoom
resources.description: Largest publicly available low-clearance dataset with 24,000+ verified clearances. Includes HeadRoom mobile app with voice-guided turn-by-turn navigation and Sentry Mode hazard monitoring. Directional metadata reduces false alarms.
resources.bestFor: Primary height-aware route planning for 12ft motorhome
resources.notes: Essential for a 12ft rig. Always add 6 inches to your actual height when entering dimensions.

resources.name: RV Life / RV Trip Wizard
resources.description: RV-specific route planner that factors in height, weight, length, and propane restrictions. Plans routes avoiding low bridges and restricted roads. Syncs between desktop and phone with Apple CarPlay support.
resources.bestFor: Pre-trip route planning with vehicle dimension safety
resources.notes: Good companion to Low Clearance Map. Plan on desktop, navigate on phone.

resources.name: Sygic Truck & RV GPS Navigation
resources.description: Plans routes excluding roads not suitable for your RV based on vehicle dimensions, weight, and cargo type. Includes offline maps.
resources.bestFor: Active turn-by-turn navigation with RV profile
resources.notes: Strong offline capability useful in remote Western US areas.

resources.name: Trucker Path
resources.description: Helps locate low-clearance bridges, truck-restricted roads, truck stops, and weigh stations. Routes suitable for large vehicles.
resources.bestFor: Supplemental bridge height reference and truck stop planning
resources.notes: Designed for truckers but very useful for large motorhomes.

resources.name: Garmin RV GPS (890/895/1095 Series)
resources.description: Dedicated RV GPS programmable for height, width, length, and weight. Automatically routes around over-limit structures including low bridges, narrow roads, and weight-restricted bridges.
resources.bestFor: Dedicated always-on dashboard navigation for motorhome
resources.notes: Many experienced RVers consider this essential. 895/1095 are current models replacing 890.

resources.name: Rand McNally Motor Carriers Road Atlas
resources.description: The trucker's bible - comprehensive road atlas with all low clearances listed by state. Works when electronics fail. Large spiral-bound version available.
features: All low clearances listed by state
resources.bestFor: Backup reference and pre-trip planning when electronics fail
resources.notes: Multiple experienced RVers recommend this as essential backup. Get the large spiral-bound version.

resources.name: Gaia GPS (with Gaia Overland Map)
resources.description: Premier overlanding GPS app with 18+ map layers. Gaia Overland map sources USFS MVUM, BLM road/trail data, and OpenStreetMap showing road surface type, vehicle constraints, closures, public land boundaries, and campsites. Entire states downloadable for offline use.
resources.bestFor: Primary adventure bike navigation and dispersed campsite scouting
resources.notes: The top choice for overlanding in the Western US. Download entire states before departing. Integrates with Trails Offroad for 2,400+ curated routes.

resources.name: onX Offroad
resources.description: 650K+ miles of motorized trails with public land boundaries, trail difficulty ratings, open/close dates, and dispersed camping layer. Offline maps with GPS navigation. Includes cell coverage layers and wildfire monitoring.
resources.bestFor: Adventure bike trail finding, land ownership verification, and dispersed camping identification
resources.notes: Excellent for adventure bike routing. The Dispersed Camping Layer uses USFS verified data. Pair with Gaia GPS for maximum coverage.

resources.name: Trails Offroad
resources.description: 2,400+ hand-curated off-road routes with detailed trip guides, difficulty ratings, and GPX downloads. Routes sourced from experienced off-road experts. Integrates directly with Gaia GPS for one-click offline use.
resources.bestFor: Finding pre-scouted adventure bike routes in Western US
resources.notes: Great for CA with ~200 tracks. Connect your Gaia GPS account for seamless route import.

resources.name: Overland Bound One
resources.description: Crowdsourced overlanding community with camping information from local experts. Includes BLM routes, USFS MVUM roads and trails, topo maps, and satellite imagery. Expedition mode offers offline navigation, track recording, and GPX import.
resources.bestFor: Community-driven route discovery and trip planning
resources.notes: Check their forums for downloadable route sections. Expedition mode membership required for full offline capability.

resources.name: CalTopo
resources.description: Advanced topographic mapping tool with public land overlays that color-code BLM, Forest Service, and other federal lands. Excellent for detailed trip planning, custom map creation, and GPX route building.
resources.bestFor: Detailed topographic trip planning and custom map creation
resources.notes: Used by SAR teams and serious backcountry travelers. Great for planning adventure bike routes with terrain detail.

resources.name: Avenza Maps
resources.description: Mobile app for viewing georeferenced PDF maps with GPS positioning. The official distribution platform for USFS Motor Vehicle Use Maps, NPS maps, and USGS topo maps. Shows your real-time location on downloaded PDF maps.
features: Map store with thousands of free maps
resources.bestFor: Viewing official USFS and BLM georeferenced PDF maps with GPS location in the field
resources.notes: Essential companion to USFS MVUMs. Search for 'MVUM' or specific ranger districts in the map store. All MVUM maps are free.

resources.name: USFS Motor Vehicle Use Maps (MVUM)
resources.description: Official free maps showing designated roads, trails, and areas open to motorized travel on all National Forests and Grasslands. Available as downloadable geospatial PDFs compatible with Avenza Maps app. Shows vehicle class restrictions, seasonal closures, and dispersed camping corridors.
features: Free download from each forest website
resources.bestFor: Legal compliance - knowing where motorized travel is permitted in National Forests
resources.notes: Download MVUMs for every National Forest along your route. If a route is not on the MVUM it is NOT open to motor vehicle travel. Use alongside a topo map for navigation.

resources.name: BLM National Data Map / Navigator
resources.description: Official BLM online map tool showing land boundaries, land status, roads, and recreation sites across all BLM-managed lands. The official source for confirming BLM land ownership and boundaries.
resources.bestFor: Confirming BLM land boundaries and finding official BLM recreation sites
resources.notes: Can be clunky but is the definitive source for BLM land verification. Use alongside onX or Gaia GPS for better interface.

resources.name: BLM Georeferenced PDF Maps
resources.description: State-specific digital maps from BLM compatible with GPS-enabled devices. Show land boundaries, roads, trails, and topography. Can be used with Avenza Maps for real-time GPS location overlay.
resources.bestFor: Offline BLM land navigation in areas without cell service
resources.notes: Download maps for AZ, CA, CO, ID, MT, NV, NM, OR, UT, WA, WY before your trip.

resources.name: USGS Topo Maps (via topoView)
resources.description: Free USGS topographic maps available for download. GeoPDF format compatible with Avenza Maps. 7.5-minute quadrangle maps cover the entire US with detailed terrain, elevation, water features, and roads.
resources.bestFor: Detailed terrain analysis for adventure bike route planning
resources.notes: Also available through Avenza Maps store. Good for planning adventure bike routes where terrain detail matters.

resources.name: USFS Interactive Visitor Map
resources.description: Interactive map to locate all campgrounds, trails, and forest roads catalogued in National Forests. Find recreation sites, visitor centers, trailheads, and points of interest.
resources.bestFor: Pre-trip research on National Forest campgrounds and trail access
resources.notes: Use for pre-trip research then download specific MVUMs for field navigation.

resources.name: Recreation.gov
resources.description: Official federal reservation system for campgrounds in National Parks, National Forests, BLM, Army Corps of Engineers, and other federal lands. Book campsites, permits, and day-use passes.
resources.bestFor: Reserving developed campgrounds in National Parks and Forests
resources.notes: Popular campgrounds book 6 months in advance. Check early for desired dates.

resources.name: iOverlander
resources.description: Free crowdsourced database of campgrounds, free camping, water, dump stations, propane, and overlander services worldwide. Popular among overlanders and international travelers.
resources.bestFor: Finding free camping and overlander services along your route
resources.notes: Web version gives full access with no account. Treat listings as lead generators - always verify with recent comments.

resources.name: Campendium
resources.description: Detailed campground review platform with cell signal information, price data, and campground tips from real users. Covers RV parks, public lands, free camping, overnight parking, and dump stations.
resources.bestFor: Researching campground quality, cell coverage, and real-world conditions
resources.notes: Pro plan adds cell coverage reports and offline access. Very strong for Western US coverage.

resources.name: FreeCampsites.net
resources.description: No-frills database of free and low-cost camping locations across the US with user reviews, photos, and GPS coordinates. Color-coded maps distinguish free sites from permit and fee sites. No account required.
resources.bestFor: Desktop/tablet pre-trip research for free camping on public land
resources.notes: Data skews toward established dispersed spots on BLM and National Forest land. Best for pre-trip planning from desktop.

resources.name: The Dyrt
resources.description: Claims largest campground database in the US. PRO adds USFS and BLM Map Layers showing free camping areas, with downloadable offline map regions. Strong photo and review coverage.
resources.bestFor: Discovering campgrounds with visual content and free camping layers
resources.notes: PRO subscription needed for the BLM/USFS map layers that make it most useful for dispersed camping.

resources.name: RVParky
resources.description: Free app with no subscription covering campgrounds, overnight parking spots, dump stations, and rest areas. Particularly strong for stops that don't show in recreation databases: city parks, fairgrounds, municipal lots.
resources.bestFor: Finding overnight stops in unfamiliar towns, especially late in the day
resources.notes: Most recommended free camping app among experienced RVers for en-route stops.

resources.name: Dispersed App
resources.description: Maps out free camping spots on BLM, USFS, and other public lands. Filter by location, see user reviews, and find GPS coordinates to verified campsites. Save locations for offline use.
resources.bestFor: Quick discovery of verified dispersed camping spots
resources.notes: Offline save feature is critical for BLM land where cell service is nonexistent.

resources.name: onX Maps (BLM Land Maps)
resources.description: Interactive BLM land maps showing 247.3 million acres of public lands with yellow shading. Includes land ownership boundaries, recreation information, and camping regulations by area.
resources.bestFor: Verifying you are on public (BLM) land before setting up camp
resources.notes: BLM lands comprise 247.3 million acres primarily in western states. Yellow shading on map denotes BLM land.

resources.name: AllStays Camp & RV
resources.description: Comprehensive directory of campgrounds, RV parks, rest stops, Walmart lots, dump stations, and low clearance alerts. Allows rig height entry. One of the most recommended all-in-one RV apps.
resources.bestFor: All-in-one big-picture trip planning with diverse stop types
resources.notes: One-time purchase, no subscription. iOS only for mobile app. Highly recommended by full-time RVers.

resources.name: Public Lands of the West Recreation Map
resources.description: Interactive map featuring recreation sites across public lands of the western United States including AZ, CA, CO, ID, MT, NV, NM, OR, UT, WA, and WY. Partnership between Public Lands Interpretive Association, USFS, and BLM.
resources.bestFor: Overview research of recreation opportunities across Western public lands
resources.notes: Covers all 11 Western states. Online store offers physical BLM and Forest Service maps.

resources.name: BLM Recreation Site Locator
resources.description: Official BLM camping information including developed campgrounds, dispersed camping guidelines, and Long-Term Visitor Areas in CA and AZ. Covers rules, stay limits, and facility information.
resources.bestFor: Official BLM camping rules and developed campground locations
resources.notes: BLM manages Long-Term Visitor Areas in CA and AZ where winter visitors can camp up to 7 months with a permit.

resources.name: DeLorme Atlas & Gazetteer
resources.description: Comprehensive state-by-state topographic map books with clear markings of public lands, back roads, and recreation areas. Excellent for identifying BLM areas, remote roads, and access points.
resources.bestFor: Backup navigation and area exploration when digital tools fail
resources.notes: Buy for each Western state you plan to visit. Many experienced overlanders carry these as essential backup.

resources.name: Benchmark Road & Recreation Atlas
resources.description: Giant map books with comprehensive topographic maps and clear markings of public lands. Excellent for finding BLM areas, dispersed camping sites, remote back roads, and access points.
resources.bestFor: Detailed area exploration and public land identification
resources.notes: Many experienced BLM campers recommend this alongside DeLorme for finding camping areas and access roads.

resources.name: Stay The Trail (Colorado MVUMs)
resources.description: Central portal for all USFS Motor Vehicle Use Maps for Colorado National Forests and Grasslands. Maps kept up to date and all available through Avenza map store for free.
resources.bestFor: One-stop download of all Colorado forest MVUMs
resources.notes: Model example of a state MVUM portal. Other states may have similar consolidated resources.

resources.name: COTREX (Colorado Trail Explorer)
resources.description: Free app from Colorado Parks and Wildlife displaying trails for hiking, biking, horseback, and OHV use. Features downloadable offline maps, ride tracking, GPS capabilities, and info on seasonal closures for 41,000+ miles of trails.
resources.bestFor: Adventure bike trail finding in Colorado
resources.notes: Free state-specific app. Check if other Western states have similar apps.

resources.name: Google Earth
resources.description: Satellite imagery tool for scouting potential campsites, access roads, and terrain before arrival. Switch between satellite and map views to assess road conditions and identify obstacles.
resources.bestFor: Pre-trip visual reconnaissance of dispersed camping areas and access roads
resources.notes: After identifying BLM areas on a map, use Google Earth to visually assess terrain and road conditions before committing.

resources.name: Roadtrippers
resources.description: Road trip planning platform with AI trip planner (Autopilot) that learns about your rig and plans RV-friendly itineraries. Discover attractions, campgrounds, and scenic routes along your path.
resources.bestFor: High-level road trip itinerary planning with points of interest
resources.notes: Good for the big-picture itinerary. Use specialized tools for detailed navigation and camping.

resources.name: US Fish & Wildlife Service Interactive Map
resources.description: Locate National Wildlife Refuges, USFWS offices, and other managed lands. Some refuges allow camping and have scenic driving routes suitable for motorhomes.
resources.bestFor: Identifying wildlife refuges along your route for camping and scenic drives
resources.notes: Some NWRs have excellent camping and wildlife viewing opportunities.

resources.name: WildernessConnect
resources.description: Service of University of Montana with information on all wilderness areas in the country. Interactive map to locate wilderness areas. Note: motorized vehicles are prohibited in wilderness areas.
resources.bestFor: Identifying wilderness boundaries to AVOID with motorized vehicles
resources.notes: IMPORTANT: Motorized vehicles are PROHIBITED in wilderness areas. Know the boundaries to avoid violations.

resources.name: Harvest Hosts
resources.description: Unique overnight RV stays at farms, wineries, breweries, and attractions. Self-contained RVs only. Great for interesting stopover locations between public land camping.
resources.bestFor: Interesting overnight stops between public land camping
resources.notes: Must be self-contained. Good way to break up long transits with unique experiences.

resources.name: KampTrail
resources.description: Built using verified federal campsite data from the Recreation.gov RIDB API rather than user-submitted content. Fills the gap left by FreeRoam's shutdown in 2024.
resources.bestFor: Finding federal campsites with verified official data
resources.notes: Newer tool built after FreeRoam shut down in 2024. Uses official Recreation.gov data.

resources.name: USFS Geospatial Data Discovery Hub
resources.description: Open data site where the public can explore Forest Service geospatial datasets by category. Build custom maps and applications using official USFS data. Includes interactive tools and ArcGIS applications.
resources.bestFor: Power users who want raw USFS data for custom mapping
resources.notes: For advanced users. Scroll to 'More Applications' section for curated interactive tools.

resources.name: Boondocking.org
resources.description: User-submitted dispersed camping spots with GPS pins. Community-driven database of free camping locations.
resources.bestFor: Supplemental source for dispersed camping locations
resources.notes: Use as supplemental source alongside Campendium and iOverlander.
